Title: Build-verification login critical-path check
Preconditions: Reference Application is deployed and reachable; valid clinician credentials exist
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to the login page | 2. Select the default session location | 3. Enter valid username and password | 4. Submit the login form
Expected_Result: Login succeeds, home dashboard renders with the selected location shown in the header

Title: Reject login when no session location is selected
Preconditions: Login page reachable with location selector present
Test_Steps: 1. Open the login page | 2. Enter valid username and password | 3. Leave the session location unselected | 4. Submit
Expected_Result: Login is blocked with a validation prompt to choose a location; no session is established

Title: Account lockout after consecutive failed attempts
Preconditions: A test account exists; lockout threshold configured (e.g., 7 attempts)
Test_Steps: 1. Submit invalid password repeatedly up to the configured threshold | 2. Observe response after threshold | 3. Attempt login with the correct password
Expected_Result: After threshold the account is locked and correct credentials are rejected until the lock window expires; lockout is audit-logged

Title: Session timeout invalidates an idle session
Preconditions: User is logged in; idle session timeout configured
Test_Steps: 1. Log in | 2. Leave the session idle past the timeout | 3. Attempt a privileged action
Expected_Result: Idle session is invalidated; user is redirected to login and the prior session token is no longer accepted

Title: Register patient critical-path build check
Preconditions: Logged in registration clerk with create-patient privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Open Register a patient | 2. Enter required demographics | 3. Enter identifier or auto-generate | 4. Confirm and save
Expected_Result: Patient is created and the patient dashboard opens with a unique identifier displayed

Title: Reject future date of birth
Preconditions: Registration form open
Test_Steps: 1. Enter valid name and gender | 2. Set DOB to tomorrow | 3. Attempt to save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked with a validation error indicating DOB cannot be in the future

Title: Estimated age boundary computes plausible DOB
Preconditions: Registration form supports estimated age entry
Test_Steps: 1. Choose estimated age | 2. Enter age 0 years | 3. Save and reopen | 4. Repeat with age 120 years
Expected_Result: Both extremes are accepted, flagged as estimated, and a computed DOB consistent with the entered age is stored

Title: Potential duplicate detection on similar demographics
Preconditions: An existing patient with known demographics exists
Test_Steps: 1. Begin registering a patient with matching name and DOB | 2. Proceed to save | 3. Observe duplicate handling
Test_Data: Match: name+DOB equal to existing; vary identifier present/absent
Expected_Result: System surfaces a potential-duplicate warning before commit; clerk can review existing match or confirm intentional create

Title: Search returns exact identifier match
Preconditions: At least one registered patient exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open Find Patient | 2. Enter the full patient identifier | 3. Submit search
Expected_Result: Single exact match is returned and selecting it opens the correct patient dashboard

Title: Name search with diacritics and partial token
Preconditions: Patients with accented names exist
Test_Steps: 1. Search using an unaccented spelling of an accented name | 2. Search using a 2-character partial token
Expected_Result: Diacritic-insensitive matching returns the expected patient; minimum-length partial search behaves per configured rule without server error

Title: Search input rejects injection-style payload
Preconditions: Find Patient search available
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a SQL/script style payload in the search box | 2. Submit
Expected_Result: No data leak or error stack is returned; payload is treated as literal text with zero or safe matches and no script execution

Title: Dashboard build-verification render
Preconditions: Existing patient with prior data; logged in clinician
Test_Steps: 1. Open an existing patient | 2. Verify header demographics, active visit banner and widget regions load
Expected_Result: Dashboard renders demographics, identifiers, and core widgets without error within acceptable load time

Title: Deceased patient banner and state indicators
Preconditions: A patient can be marked deceased
Test_Steps: 1. Mark a test patient deceased with date and cause | 2. Reopen the dashboard | 3. Revert deceased flag where permitted
Expected_Result: Deceased state shows a clear banner and death date; reverting clears the banner and restores active-state widgets

Title: Start active visit critical-path check
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; patient with no active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient | 2. Start a visit | 3. Select visit type and location | 4. Confirm
Expected_Result: An active visit is created with start datetime, type and location and an active-visit banner appears

Title: Prevent overlapping active visits for one patient
Preconditions: Patient already has an active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient with an active visit | 2. Attempt to start a second concurrent visit
Expected_Result: System prevents a second overlapping active visit or requires closing the current one first; no duplicate active visit is created

Title: Visit lifecycle state transitions
Preconditions: Patient has an active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Add an encounter to the active visit | 2. End the visit | 3. Attempt to add an encounter to the ended visit
Expected_Result: Active to ended transition is recorded with stop datetime; adding encounters to an ended visit is blocked or requires reopening per policy

Title: Capture vitals encounter build check
Preconditions: Clinician with an active visit on the patient
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient with an active visit | 2. Launch the vitals form | 3. Enter pulse, BP, temperature, SpO2 | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Vitals encounter saves and appears under recent encounters with correct datetime and provider

Title: Out-of-range vital triggers validation or alert
Preconditions: Vitals form with configured absolute ranges
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a physiologically impossible value | 2. Enter an extreme-but-valid critical value | 3. Attempt to save each
Expected_Result: Impossible value is rejected by absolute-range validation; critical-but-valid value is accepted and visibly flagged abnormal

Title: BMI auto-calculation decision table
Preconditions: Vitals form computes BMI from height and weight
Test_Steps: 1. Enter height and weight pairs | 2. Observe computed BMI and category
Expected_Result: Valid pair yields correct BMI and category; missing/zero height or weight yields no BMI without error

Title: Add drug allergy with severity and reaction
Preconditions: Clinician on patient chart with allergy module
Test_Steps: 1. Open Allergies | 2. Add a drug allergen | 3. Set reaction and severity | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Allergy is recorded and listed with allergen, reaction and severity; appears in the active allergy list

Title: No known allergies versus unknown state
Preconditions: Patient with empty allergy list
Test_Steps: 1. Mark No Known Allergies | 2. Attempt to add a specific allergy afterward | 3. Resolve the conflict
Expected_Result: Marking NKA sets an explicit asserted state distinct from unknown; adding a specific allergy resolves NKA and updates the list consistently

Title: Code a coded diagnosis as primary versus secondary
Preconditions: Active visit; diagnosis entry available
Test_Steps: 1. Search and select a coded diagnosis | 2. Mark certainty confirmed | 3. Set rank primary | 4. Add a second as secondary
Expected_Result: Both diagnoses save with correct rank and certainty; only one primary is allowed per encounter per configured rule

Title: Book appointment critical-path check
Preconditions: Appointment scheduling module enabled; provider and service exist
Test_Steps: 1. Open appointment scheduling | 2. Select patient, service and provider | 3. Pick an available slot | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Appointment is booked in scheduled status and appears on the provider and patient schedules

Title: Double-booking and slot conflict handling
Preconditions: An appointment already occupies a provider slot
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to book a second appointment in the same provider slot | 2. Observe conflict handling
Expected_Result: System warns or prevents overlapping booking per configured policy; no silent double-book occurs

Title: Appointment status state machine
Preconditions: A scheduled appointment exists
Test_Steps: 1. Transition Scheduled to CheckedIn | 2. CheckedIn to Completed | 3. Attempt Completed to Scheduled
Test_Data: States: Scheduled, CheckedIn, Completed, Cancelled, Missed
Expected_Result: Forward transitions succeed and timestamp; illegal backward transition from Completed is rejected; cancel/missed paths are recorded

Title: Place a drug order critical-path check
Preconditions: Provider with ordering privilege; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open Orders | 2. Add a drug order | 3. Enter dose, route, frequency and duration | 4. Sign and save
Expected_Result: Order is created in active status under the encounter with the full dosing instruction and ordering provider

Title: Discontinue and revise order state transitions
Preconditions: An active drug order exists
Test_Steps: 1. Revise the active order creating a new revision | 2. Discontinue the order | 3. Attempt to revise the discontinued order
Expected_Result: Revision supersedes the prior order with linkage; discontinue sets stopped state; revising a discontinued order is blocked

Title: Allergy-interaction safety check on ordering
Preconditions: Patient has a recorded drug allergy
Test_Steps: 1. Order a drug matching the recorded allergen class | 2. Order an unrelated drug | 3. Observe alerting
Expected_Result: Ordering the conflicting drug raises a safety alert requiring acknowledgement; the unrelated drug raises no allergy alert

Title: Place lab order and result lifecycle
Preconditions: Lab ordering and result entry enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Order a lab test | 2. Mark collected | 3. Enter a result value | 4. Verify result appears on chart
Expected_Result: Lab order moves ordered to collected to resulted; the result links to the originating order and shows on the patient chart

Title: Radiology order with abnormal flag handling
Preconditions: Radiology ordering available
Test_Steps: 1. Place an imaging order | 2. Enter a result with an abnormal/critical flag | 3. Verify chart presentation
Expected_Result: Imaging order and result save; critical-flagged result is visually distinguished on the chart and in the results list

Title: Dispense against an active drug order
Preconditions: Pharmacy module enabled; an active drug order exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the dispensing queue | 2. Select the active order | 3. Record dispensed quantity | 4. Confirm dispense
Expected_Result: Dispense is recorded against the order, decrements remaining quantity, and is auditable to the dispensing user

Title: Reject dispense quantity exceeding ordered amount
Preconditions: An active order with a finite quantity exists
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to dispense more than the ordered quantity | 2. Attempt to dispense a negative quantity
Expected_Result: Over-dispense and negative quantity are rejected with validation; no inventory or order change is committed

Title: Privilege enforcement across roles pairwise
Preconditions: Users exist for clerk, nurse, physician, pharmacist roles
Test_Steps: 1. For each role attempt register, order, dispense, configure-system | 2. Record allow/deny
Expected_Result: Each role is permitted only its authorized actions and denied others with a clear authorization message; no privilege escalation

Title: Direct API access respects role privileges
Preconditions: A low-privilege user token and a privileged endpoint exist
Test_Steps: 1. Call a privileged REST endpoint with a low-privilege session | 2. Compare with a privileged session
Expected_Result: Low-privilege call is rejected (403) and the privileged call succeeds; UI-hidden actions are not reachable via API

Title: Merge two patient records preserves clinical data
Preconditions: Two duplicate patient records with distinct encounters exist
Test_Steps: 1. Initiate patient merge selecting surviving record | 2. Confirm merge | 3. Verify encounters, orders and identifiers on survivor
Expected_Result: All clinical data consolidates onto the survivor; non-survivor is voided with audit trail; no orphaned encounters remain

Title: Audit log captures PHI access events
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Open a patient chart as a known user | 2. Modify a clinical record | 3. Inspect the audit log
Expected_Result: Audit log records access and change events with user, timestamp, patient and action; entries are immutable to standard users

Title: Run a report build-verification check
Preconditions: Reporting module configured with at least one report
Test_Steps: 1. Open Reports | 2. Run a predefined report with a date range | 3. View output
Expected_Result: Report executes and returns results consistent with the date range without error

Title: FHIR metadata CapabilityStatement build check
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled
Test_Steps: 1. GET the /fhir2/R4/metadata endpoint | 2. Inspect resource and status
Expected_Result: HTTP 200 returns a CapabilityStatement listing supported resources and interactions in valid FHIR R4 JSON

Title: Read Patient resource and validate mandatory elements
Preconditions: A patient exists with a FHIR id
Test_Steps: 1. GET /fhir2/R4/Patient/{id} | 2. Validate identifier, name and gender elements | 3. Request unsupported format
Expected_Result: Patient resource returns with required elements correctly mapped; unsupported format negotiation returns a proper FHIR OperationOutcome

Title: Search Observation by patient and code
Preconditions: Vitals observations exist for a patient
Test_Steps: 1. GET /fhir2/R4/Observation?patient={id}&code={loinc} | 2. Validate bundle and codings
Expected_Result: A searchset Bundle returns matching Observations with correct LOINC coding and value/unit; empty search returns an empty Bundle not an error

Title: Unauthenticated FHIR access is rejected
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint requires authentication
Test_Steps: 1. GET a FHIR resource with no/invalid credentials | 2. GET with valid credentials
Expected_Result: Unauthenticated request returns 401/403 with no PHI in the body; authenticated request returns the resource

Title: HL7 v2 ADT^A01 admit message ingestion
Preconditions: HL7 v2 inbound interface configured
Test_Steps: 1. Submit a well-formed ADT^A01 admit message | 2. Verify patient/visit creation or update | 3. Submit a malformed segment
Test_Data: MsgType=ADT^A01; PID populated; then corrupt PID
Expected_Result: Valid A01 creates/updates the patient and visit and returns an ACK; malformed message returns an NACK/AE and creates no partial record

Title: HL7 ORU^R01 result message maps to observations
Preconditions: HL7 result inbound interface configured; matching patient/order exists
Test_Steps: 1. Submit an ORU^R01 with OBX result segments | 2. Verify observations attach to the correct patient and order
Expected_Result: Result observations are created with correct value, units and code and link to the matching order; an ACK is returned

Title: Login and registration keyboard and label conformance
Preconditions: WCAG 2.1 AA target; screen reader available
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate login and registration using keyboard only | 2. Verify visible focus, label associations and error announcements
Expected_Result: All interactive controls are keyboard reachable with visible focus, programmatic labels, and errors announced to assistive technology

Title: Critical lab result generates clinician notification
Preconditions: Notification/alerting configured for critical results
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a critical-flagged lab result | 2. Verify a notification is generated to the responsible clinician | 3. Acknowledge
Expected_Result: A notification is dispatched to the responsible clinician and acknowledgement is recorded with timestamp

Title: Charge generated from a billable service
Preconditions: Billing module enabled; chargeable service configured
Test_Steps: 1. Perform a billable encounter or order | 2. Open the billing/charges view | 3. Verify line item
Expected_Result: A charge line item is generated for the billable service with correct amount and linkage to the encounter

Title: Telehealth visit link generation and access control
Preconditions: Telemedicine module enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Schedule a telehealth visit | 2. Generate the session link | 3. Attempt access without authorization
Expected_Result: A unique session link is generated; unauthorized access without a valid invite/credential is denied

Title: Cross-module patient journey exploratory pass
Preconditions: Full environment available with a fresh test patient
Test_Steps: 1. Register patient | 2. Start visit | 3. Capture vitals | 4. Place orders | 5. Resolve results | 6. End visit and review chart
Expected_Result: No broken transitions, data loss, or inconsistency across modules; chart accurately reflects every action with correct timestamps and provenance

Title: Logout terminates session and protects back-navigation
Preconditions: User is logged in on a patient chart
Test_Steps: 1. Log out | 2. Use browser back to a prior PHI page | 3. Attempt a server action
Test_Data: Action=back to patient chart after logout
Expected_Result: Logout invalidates the session; back-navigation does not expose PHI and any server action redirects to login

Title: Concept and identifier data integrity check
Preconditions: Database read access for verification
Test_Steps: 1. Create a patient with an identifier | 2. Query persistence for the identifier and demographics | 3. Verify no truncation or encoding loss
Expected_Result: Persisted record matches input exactly with correct encoding, referential integrity to person and no orphan rows
